description: Core game engine: card types, deck construction, legal move generation, game loop, trick resolution, jing gong. Foundation for all AI work.
title: Game Engine

close_reason: Implemented Card, Rank, Suit, Deck types in src/zsy/cards.py with 29 passing tests
description: Define card types (rank, suit), deck construction for single-deck (54 cards) and double-deck (108 cards). Handle joker types (small/large). Represent cards as immutable value objects.
title: Card and deck representation

close_reason: Team mode in game.py with 6 tests
description: Team assignment and win condition: if one player on a team wins, the whole team wins. Support configurable team compositions. Track team membership in game state.
title: Team mode support

close_reason: 184 total tests covering all combination types, bombs, wildcards, jokers, multi-deck, dynamic ranking, legal moves, team mode, jing gong, and integration. All pass in <1s.
description: Comprehensive unit tests: all combination types, bomb hierarchy, wildcard in every combo type, dynamic ranking edge cases, legal move generation correctness, joker rules, multi-deck scenarios, jing gong, team win conditions. Property-based tests for legal move generator.
title: Game engine test suite

close_reason: RandomAgent in src/zsy/agents.py with 3 tests, stable across seeds for 2-4 players
description: Agent that selects uniformly from legal moves. Used for sanity-checking the game engine and as the weakest baseline for AI evaluation.
title: Random-play baseline agent

close_reason: Player state in src/zsy/player.py with 10 tests
description: Per-player state: hand, high-card rank (starts at 2, increments on win), team assignment, cards-remaining count. Track high-card rank independently across games.
title: Player state model

close_reason: Combination types in src/zsy/combinations.py with 43 tests covering all types + bomb hierarchy
description: Define all legal combination types: single, pair, triple, triple+pair (5 cards), straight (exactly 5), consecutive pairs (exactly 6), consecutive triples (exactly 6), bombs (4+ of a kind), straight flush (exactly 5). Validate combinations against rules. Handle joker pairs (must be same type).
title: Combination types and validation

close_reason: Dynamic ranking in src/zsy/ranking.py with 21 tests
description: Implement per-player card ranking based on their current high-card rank. The high card of hearts outranks same-rank cards of other suits. Ranking wraps/increments correctly. Comparison operators for all combination types respect per-player rankings.
title: Dynamic card ranking system

close_reason: Wildcard substitution in src/zsy/wildcard.py with 12 tests
description: Heart-suit high card functions as wildcard: can substitute for any card in any combination including straights, straight flushes, and bombs. Generate all valid wildcard substitution options. Wildcard-containing bombs and straight flushes are still valid bombs.
title: Wildcard mechanics

close_reason: Legal move generator in src/zsy/legal_moves.py with 14 tests
description: Given a hand, current trick type (or free lead), and wildcard availability: enumerate all legal plays including pass. Handle all combination types, wildcard substitutions, bomb hierarchy (straight flush > 5-of-a-kind, but not > 6-of-a-kind). Must be fast — this is called millions of times during training.
title: Legal move generator

close_reason: Already implemented in combinations.py: _bomb_power, _bomb_beats, Combination.beats. Bomb hierarchy tested in test_combinations.py (straight flush > 5-of-a-kind, 6-of-a-kind > straight flush, etc.). Wildcard bombs use same comparison via classify.
description: Implement bomb ordering: higher count beats lower count. Straight flush beats 5-of-a-kind but not 6+. Within same type, higher rank wins. Bombs beat all non-bomb combinations. Handle comparison correctly with wildcards.
title: Bomb hierarchy and comparison

close_reason: Game loop in src/zsy/game.py, optimized legal_moves.py. 14 game tests + 14 legal move tests, all passing. Games complete in <0.1s for 2-4 players.
description: Full game loop: deal cards evenly, players take turns playing or passing, trick resolution (last uncontested player leads next), round ends when a player empties hand. Support variable player count (2-6+). Pass-and-return semantics (can re-enter trick after passing).
title: Game loop and trick resolution

close_reason: Jing Gong exchange in src/zsy/jing_gong.py with 8 tests
description: Post-round card exchange: loser gives their best card to winner, winner returns any card below 10. Implement as a separate decision phase. Handle edge cases (what if loser has no cards above winner's return constraint).
title: Jing gong card exchange

description: Perfect-Training-Imperfect-Execution enhancement. Perfect-info critic, min-steps-to-shed oracle, PPO+GAE training. PerfectDou-style 10x sample efficiency boost.
title: PTIE + Oracle Rewards

description: BFS/DP over hand decompositions to compute minimum number of plays to shed all cards. Must handle all combo types, wildcards. Cache common hands. This provides the dense oracle reward signal for PTIE training.
title: Min-steps-to-shed oracle

description: Value network that sees ALL players' cards during training. Input: full game state + all hidden hands + min-steps-to-shed per player. Output: state value estimate. Architecture: similar MLP to Q-network but with expanded input.
title: Perfect-info critic network

description: Replace DMC with PPO + Generalized Advantage Estimation. Actor (policy) sees imperfect info only. Critic sees perfect info (PTIE). Dense oracle reward: advantage based on min-steps difference between players. Compare sample efficiency vs DMC baseline.
title: PPO + GAE training with PTIE

description: Rigorous comparison: PTIE (PPO+oracle) vs DMC baseline at fixed compute budgets. Measure WP, ADP, sample efficiency, training wall time. Run ablations: PTIE without oracle reward, oracle reward without PTIE.
title: PTIE vs DMC comparison

description: Behavioral regularization for team cooperation (GuanZero-style), CTDE training, Transformer-based opponent belief network, belief-integrated Q-network.
title: Team Play + Opponent Modeling

description: Transformer encoder (4 layers, 8 heads) that predicts per-opponent card distributions from public game state and play history sequence. Input: played cards, pass patterns, card counts. Output: probability over RxS per opponent. Train supervised from self-play data.
title: Opponent belief network

description: GuanZero-style: add ~9D one-hot flags to state encoding: can-cooperate, play-defensively, assist-teammate. These flags encode cooperative intent explicitly rather than hoping the network discovers cooperation. Train flags via heuristic rules initially, then refine.
title: Behavioral regularization for team play

description: Centralized Training, Decentralized Execution: during training, team agents share full information (all teammates' hands). During inference, each agent acts on own info only. Implement shared critic for teammates, independent actors.
title: CTDE training infrastructure

description: Augment Q-network state input with opponent belief network outputs. The belief distribution over opponent hands becomes additional state features. Fine-tune existing trained Q-network with belief features. Measure improvement over belief-free baseline.
title: Belief-integrated Q-network

description: Evaluate team AI: 2v2 and 3v3 tournaments. Compare CTDE+behavioral-reg vs naive independent agents. Measure team win rate, cooperation quality (does the AI make sacrificial plays for teammates?).
title: Team mode evaluation

description: IS-MCTS with neural guidance at decision time, cross-game meta-strategy network, jing gong exchange network. Final push toward near-optimal play.
title: Search + Meta-Strategy

description: At decision time: sample N determinizations (opponent hands consistent with beliefs), run short lookahead with Q-network as leaf evaluator, aggregate across determinizations. Tunable compute budget (N, search depth). Should improve over pure Q-network policy.
title: IS-MCTS with neural guidance

description: Small dedicated network for the post-round card exchange decision. Winner must choose which card below 10 to return. Input: winner's hand, loser's known info, game meta-state. Output: card selection policy. Train from self-play jing gong episodes.
title: Jing gong exchange network

description: Meta-level network that considers all players' high-card ranks and game history to adjust per-game strategy. Input: all players' current supreme ranks, win/loss history, standings. Output: reward adjustment weight (how much to value winning this specific game). Addresses strategic losing scenarios.
title: Cross-game meta-strategy network

description: Integrate all components: Q-network + belief network + IS-MCTS + jing gong network + meta-strategy. End-to-end pipeline: meta-strategy adjusts reward -> PTIE trains per-game policy -> IS-MCTS refines at decision time -> jing gong handles exchanges.
title: Full system integration

description: Deep Monte Carlo self-play RL with action-in Q-network. State/action encoding, LSTM+MLP architecture, multi-process training, evaluation harness.
title: DouZero-Style RL Baseline

description: Encode game state as tensors: current hand (RxS matrix with counts), played cards per player, last K moves (K~15-20), cards remaining per player, high-card rank per player (one-hot), wildcard status, bomb count, team flags. ~600D+ vector. Support single and double deck.
title: State encoding

description: Encode actions as RxS card matrices (DouZero-style action-in architecture). Wildcard substitutions encoded by placing card in assigned position. Pass encoded as zero matrix. Must enable Q-network generalization across structurally similar combinations.
title: Action encoding

description: LSTM (hidden_dim=256) for move history encoding + 6-layer MLP (hidden_dim=512, ReLU). Input: concatenated [state_features, action_features]. Output: scalar Q-value. Separate networks per player-count configuration. PyTorch implementation.
title: Q-network architecture

description: Multi-process actor system: M actor processes generate episodes via epsilon-greedy self-play (epsilon=0.01). Each episode records all (state, action, return) tuples. Episodes sent to central learner via shared memory or message queue. Start with M=8-16 actors.
title: Self-play episode generation

description: Deep Monte Carlo learner: consume episodes from actors, compute every-visit MC returns (gamma=1, sparse terminal reward), update Q-network via MSE loss. Implement learning rate schedule, gradient clipping, periodic model checkpointing.
title: DMC training loop

description: Round-robin tournament system: pit AI versions against each other and baselines. Track Win Probability (WP), Average Differential Points (ADP), Elo rating. 10K+ game tournaments for statistical significance. Separate eval per player count and mode.
title: Evaluation harness

description: Stronger baseline than random: prioritize shedding (play smallest legal combo that beats current trick), save bombs for later, prefer to keep hand flexible. Used as mid-tier evaluation baseline.
title: Rule-based heuristic agent

description: Train initial model on simplified rules (3-player, no teams, no wildcard). Then incrementally add: wildcard, dynamic high card, multi-deck, variable player count. Verify each addition improves or maintains performance.
title: Incremental rule integration

description: Inference optimization, legal-move-gen C/Rust bindings, hyperparameter tuning, ablation studies, final evaluation across all player counts and modes.
title: Optimization + Evaluation

description: Profile legal move gen (called millions of times during training). If Python is bottleneck, rewrite in C/Rust with Python bindings (pyo3 or cffi). Target: <100μs per call for typical hand sizes.
title: Legal move generation optimization

description: Profile and optimize inference: batch legal action evaluation, ONNX/TorchScript export, GPU batching for IS-MCTS determinizations. Target: <10ms per decision for real-time play.
title: Inference latency optimization

description: Systematic tuning: learning rate, epsilon, LSTM hidden dim, MLP depth/width, PPO clip ratio, GAE lambda, number of actors, search depth/width for IS-MCTS. Use population-based training or grid search.
title: Hyperparameter tuning

description: Measure contribution of each component: PTIE on/off, oracle reward on/off, belief network on/off, IS-MCTS on/off, behavioral reg on/off, meta-strategy on/off. Full factorial not needed — test each component independently.
title: Ablation studies

description: Comprehensive eval: all player counts (2-6), team vs FFA, various high-card rank configurations, single vs double deck. 10K+ games per config. Report WP, ADP, Elo. Compare full system vs each phase's best model.
title: Final cross-configuration evaluation
